events.event_description: First U.S. consulate opened in Hamburg, one of the three Hanseatic capital cities, establishing the first formal U.S. presence in the Hanseatic Republics.

evidence.quote: The first U.S. Consulate to open in Hamburg was on June 17, 1790. It closed on January 2, 1967.

events.event_description: The United States established diplomatic relations with the Hanseatic Republics, elevating representation to legation level when it received Rudolph Schleiden as Minister Resident of the Hanseatic Legation in Washington D.C.

evidence.quote: The United States established diplomatic relations with the Hanseatic Republics in October 1853 when it received Rudolph Schleiden as Minister Resident of the Hanseatic Legation in Washington D.C.

events.event_description: Diplomatic relations severed as the Hanseatic Republics joined the North German Confederation. The Hanseatic mission was withdrawn when Acting Chargé d'Affaires Schumacker presented the letters of recall of the Hanseatic Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: On September 25, 1868, the Hanseatic Republic Acting Chargé d'Affaires A. Schumacker presented the letters of recall of Dr. Johannes Rosing, the Hanseatic Republic's Chargé d'Affaires. The withdrawal of the Hanseatic mission was due to the three Republics (Bremen, Hamburg, and Lübeck) joining the North German Union.
